channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: WNBA draftees turn attention to making rosters as training camp opens in a few weeks
item.description: Paige Bueckers will be heading to Dallas, the latest transcendent college star to go No. 1 in the WNBA draft.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Concerns mount at Red Bull after Verstappen's 'catastrophe' as F1 heads to Saudi Arabia
item.description: Max Verstappen and Red Bull are one of Formula 1's all-time most successful partnerships. But a disastrous race has raised concerns Verstappen could consider a move elsewhere. Following the Bahrain Grand Prix, Verstappen talked of a “catastrophe” as he listed the problems.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ELLINGWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Champions League: PSG and Barcelona protect leads heading into 2nd legs of quarterfinals
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, England (AP) — After shedding its “galácticos,” Paris Saint-Germain looks in better shape than ever to win the Champions League and can reach the semifinals on Tuesday by completing the job against Aston Villa.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Roster limit changes not offered in latest filing on $2.8 billion NCAA lawsuit settlement
item.description: Attorneys working on the legal settlement designed to reshape college sports filed a brief that did not offer changes a judge suggested regarding roster limits, reiterating the benefit the limits provide to some athletes and the havoc a late change to that rule could create.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sky reunite Hailey Van Lith with Angel Reese in WNBA Draft
item.description: Hailey Van Lith and Angel Reese are teammates once more, as the Chicago Sky took the former LSU Tigers guard 11th overall in the 2025 WNBA Draft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pelicans fire basketball operations chief David Griffin after an injury-plagued 21-61 season
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The New Orleans Pelicans fired basketball operations chief David Griffin on Monday, ending a six-year chapter during which the club drafted former Duke superstar Zion Williamson but still struggled to win consistently with three different coaches.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump praises Ohio State Buckeyes during football title ceremony at White House
item.description: President Donald Trump sang high praise for the Ohio State Buckeyes during their national title celebration at the White House on Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Miami's Mario Cristobal delivers stern message to players thinking of NIL holdouts
item.description: Miami Hurricanes football coach Mario Cristobal sent a warning to players who were thinking of trying to pull an NIL holdout similar to what was seen in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Patrick Reed stuns himself in Masters final round with eagle on 17th hole
item.description: Patrick Reed finished in third place at the Masters on Sunday, but it was his shot on 17 that stunned everyone, including himself. Reed asked whether the ball went in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jazz close out season with 116-105 loss at Minnesota
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (ABC4 Sports) - The NBA season has mercifully come to an end for the Utah Jazz. The Jazz ended the worst season in franchise history with a 116-105 loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves, who were led by Anthony Edwards with 43 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Crow-Armstrong homers twice and Cubs take series with 4-2 victory over Dodgers
item.description: Pete Crow-Armstrong hit his first two home runs of the season, including a go-ahead solo shot in the seventh inning, Michael Busch homered against his former team again and the Chicago Cubs beat the Los Angeles Dodgers 4-2 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dodgers host the Cubs on 7-game home win streak
item.description: Chicago Cubs (9-7, second in the NL Central) vs. Los Angeles Dodgers (11-4, third in the NL West) Los Angeles; Saturday, 9:10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fruita and Palisade show off defensive skill in 2-1 Wildcats win
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — We had an absolute showdown between two worthy Southwestern League foes at Suplizio Field Friday. Fruita pitchers Gavin Cardoza (1.62 ERA Friday) and Noah Brackett (0 ERA Friday) shut down Palisade's offense after a first-inning run.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Blackhawks host the Jets following Foligno's 2-goal game
item.description: Winnipeg Jets (54-21-4, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (23-46-10, in the Central Division) Chicago; Saturday, 7 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks host the Winnipeg Jets after Nick Foligno's two-goal game against the Boston Bruins in the Blackhawks' 5-2 win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox enter matchup with the Red Sox on losing streak
item.description: Boston Red Sox (7-7, third in the AL East) vs. Chicago White Sox (2-10, fifth in the AL Central) Chicago; Friday, 7:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Blackhawks score 4 3rd-period goals for 5-2 win over Bruins
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Nick Foligno scored a pair of goals, Arvid Soderblom made 31 saves and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Boston Bruins 5-2 on Thursday night. Ryan Donato, Tyler Bertuzzi and Kevin Korchinski also had goals for the Blackhawks, who scored four goals in third period to pull away.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Swanson hits tiebreaking single in 4-run eighth as the Cubs beat the Rangers 10-6
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Dansby Swanson lined a tiebreaking two-run single in Chicago's four-run eighth inning, and the Cubs beat the Texas Rangers 10-6 on Tuesday on another frigid night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
